title: 5 Tests to Recognize God’s Voice
hook: If it’s God, it will pass these 5 tests.
script: If it’s God, it will pass these 5 tests. One, it agrees with Scripture; God won’t contradict His Word (2 Tim 3:16). Two, it sounds like Jesus and leads toward love, joy, and self-control (Jn 10:27; Gal 5). Three, it brings steady peace with practical clarity, not panic (Col 3:15). Four, wise believers can confirm it (Prov 11:14). Five, good fruit shows up over time (Mt 7:16). Use a voice log: Date, what I sensed, Bible anchor, counsel, peace level, fruit. Then wait 24 hours before acting, unless it’s obvious obedience. Take heart—His sheep hear His voice; He knows yours by name (Jn 10:27).

title: When God Feels Silent: Test the Voice
hook: If God feels silent right now, watch this.
script: If God feels silent right now, watch this. Start a 24-hour pause. Write what you sensed in a voice log: what I heard, Scripture check, counsel to ask, peace level, possible fruit. Now test it. Does it match the Bible’s heart and words (2 Tim 3:16)? Does it sound like the Shepherd, not shame or fear (Jn 10:27; Gal 5)? Do you feel peace that guides next steps (Col 3:15)? Do trusted believers nod yes (Prov 11:14)? Over time, does it produce good fruit (Mt 7:16)? You don’t have to force it. Jesus knows how to lead you. His sheep hear His voice, and that includes you.

title: Stop Overthinking: Simple Tests for God’s Voice
script: You may be overthinking this. God’s voice is simpler than you think. Say the thought out loud and ask five questions. Does Scripture support it, not just a verse grabbed out of context (2 Tim 3:16)? Does it sound like Jesus and grow love, joy, and self-control (Jn 10:27; Gal 5)? Do you sense peace with clear next steps (Col 3:15)? Would wise counsel agree (Prov 11:14)? If you wait 24 hours, does good fruit keep pointing the same way (Mt 7:16)? Log it, pray, then obey. If you miss it, grace covers you. The Shepherd is better at speaking than we are at hearing. His sheep hear His voice (Jn 10:27).
